messages.content:
A hidden rule transforms sequences of 6 items by chaining exactly two operations. Allowed operations are cycle-advance, full reversal, a fixed-position swap, a fixed-position overwrite, and a first-item-conditional one-step rotation. The examples determine the requested output.
Cycle order: XI XII IX VI VIII I (after the last item, wrap to the first).
Probe log:
  XI / I / XII / I / VI / VIII -> I / VIII / XI / IX / XI / XII
  I / VIII / XII / I / VI / XII -> IX / VIII / XI / IX / I / XI
  I / VI / XII / VI / IX / VIII -> I / VI / VIII / IX / VIII / XI
  XI / XII / IX / XII / VI / XII -> IX / VIII / IX / VI / IX / XII
  I / XI / VIII / I / VI / VI -> VIII / VIII / XI / I / XII / XI
  VI / VIII / VIII / IX / I / XI -> XII / XI / VI / I / I / VIII

Find the output for XII / IX / IX / IX / XI / XII.
End with exactly one line:
ANSWER: <answer>

Maintain three registers. Initial state: gim=1, jor=7, bex=5. Apply each instruction to the current state in order.
  1. cap bex at 9
  2. if bex is at least 8, add 2 to gim
  3. if jor is at least 8, add 2 to gim
  4. move up to 2 from jor to bex
  5. if gim is at least 3, add 2 to jor
  6. move up to 3 from gim to jor
End with exactly one line:
ANSWER: <answer> (format name=value entries in the original name order, separated by ';')

Choose the single probe that best distinguishes these three candidate rules. Score a probe by how many distinct outputs the hypotheses predict; choose the unique maximum.
Hypotheses:
  H1: if the first item is VIII or II, rotate left once; otherwise rotate right once
  H2: overwrite position 4 with III
  H3: if the first item is VII or II, rotate left once; otherwise rotate right once
Candidate probes:
  P1: IV / III / V / IV / IV
  P2: VII / IX / IX / IX / VII
  P3: IX / VI / III / IX / II
Cycle order: III VIII V IX VII II IV VI (after the last item, wrap to the first).
End with exactly one line: ANSWER: P1, ANSWER: P2, or ANSWER: P3

Audit this worked procedure. It starts at 91 ~ 35 ~ 68 ~ 35 ~ 35 ~ 02, contains exactly one first error, and later lines continue from the erroneous state. Locate the first error, recompute from there, and return the correct final state.
  step 1 (advance every item 6 step(s) in the cycle): 91 ~ 35 ~ 68 ~ 35 ~ 35 ~ 02 -> 79 ~ 91 ~ 12 ~ 91 ~ 91 ~ 34
  step 2 (reverse the entire sequence): 79 ~ 91 ~ 12 ~ 91 ~ 91 ~ 34 -> 34 ~ 91 ~ 91 ~ 12 ~ 91 ~ 79
  step 3 (advance every item 3 step(s) in the cycle): 34 ~ 91 ~ 91 ~ 12 ~ 91 ~ 79 -> 68 ~ 68 ~ 02 ~ 91 ~ 02 ~ 34
  step 4 (reverse the entire sequence): 68 ~ 68 ~ 02 ~ 91 ~ 02 ~ 34 -> 34 ~ 02 ~ 91 ~ 02 ~ 68 ~ 68
Cycle order: 91 35 34 02 12 68 79 (after the last item, wrap to the first).
End with exactly one line:
ANSWER: <answer>

Choose the lowest-cost tool that supports every required capability.
Tools:
  - 12: supports image,stream,table,text; cost 1
  - 91: supports exact,table,text; cost 6
  - 35: supports exact,image,stream,table; cost 5
  - 80: supports exact,stream,table; cost 6
  - 68: supports exact,image,text; cost 2
Required: image,table,text.
End with exactly one line:
ANSWER: <answer>

A worker starts with VIII ~ VIII ~ VIII ~ VIII ~ VI ~ V ~ VI and applies: reverse the entire sequence; then swap positions 2 and 3; then swap positions 1 and 3. They claim the result is V ~ VI ~ VI ~ VIII ~ V ~ VIII ~ VIII. Verify the claim.
Cycle order: XII V II IV VI VIII (after the last item, wrap to the first).
End with exactly one line: ANSWER: YES or ANSWER: NO

A hidden rule transforms sequences of 7 items by chaining exactly two operations. Allowed operations are cycle-advance, full reversal, a fixed-position swap, a fixed-position overwrite, and a first-item-conditional one-step rotation. The examples determine the requested output.
Cycle order: H D T J A P M K R F (after the last item, wrap to the first).
Probe log:
  A ~ A ~ P ~ R ~ D ~ H ~ D -> D ~ H ~ A ~ R ~ P ~ A ~ D
  D ~ H ~ R ~ A ~ R ~ T ~ K -> K ~ T ~ D ~ A ~ R ~ H ~ R
  P ~ T ~ F ~ P ~ P ~ A ~ P -> P ~ A ~ P ~ P ~ F ~ T ~ P
  M ~ K ~ F ~ F ~ K ~ R ~ T -> T ~ R ~ M ~ F ~ F ~ K ~ K
  J ~ A ~ K ~ D ~ T ~ M ~ H -> H ~ M ~ J ~ D ~ K ~ A ~ T
  R ~ K ~ J ~ P ~ R ~ M ~ T -> T ~ M ~ R ~ P ~ J ~ K ~ R

Find the output for F ~ K ~ K ~ R ~ D ~ K ~ M.
End with exactly one line:
ANSWER: <answer>

A hidden rule transforms sequences of 7 items by chaining exactly two operations. Allowed operations are cycle-advance, full reversal, a fixed-position swap, a fixed-position overwrite, and a first-item-conditional one-step rotation. The examples determine the requested output.
Cycle order: I X XI VIII IV VII IX V III VI (after the last item, wrap to the first).
Probe log:
  VIII · VIII · VII · VIII · III · VIII · VI -> X · IX · VIII · X · X · X · V
  VI · X · VIII · I · III · VIII · VI -> V · IX · X · III · VI · X · V
  VII · III · VI · VII · XI · VII · IV -> VIII · I · V · VIII · IX · VIII · XI
  I · VI · IV · VIII · VIII · VIII · VII -> III · X · XI · X · V · X · VIII
  XI · IV · VI · IX · XI · V · IV -> I · I · V · IV · XI · VII · XI
  X · I · X · I · X · III · V -> VI · VI · VI · III · III · IX · VII

Find the output for III · VIII · VI · III · I · V · V.
End with exactly one line:
ANSWER: <answer>

Choose the lowest-cost tool that supports every required capability.
Tools:
  - per: supports image,stream,text; cost 9
  - vom: supports exact,text; cost 9
  - min: supports image,stream,table,text; cost 6
  - dom: supports image,stream; cost 1
  - pan: supports stream,text; cost 5
Required: stream.
End with exactly one line:
ANSWER: <answer>

Return an id only when exactly one record satisfies all requirements. If none or more than one qualifies, abstain with INSUFFICIENT.
Records:

  - T: mass 3, glow 3, span 5, charge 8
  - H: mass 6, glow 8, span 3, charge 3
  - P: mass 2, glow 2, span 4, charge 3
  - W: mass 5, glow 3, span 9, charge 2
  - M: mass 2, glow 5, span 5, charge 7
Requirements: mass <= 4 and glow <= 5 and span <= 5.
End with exactly one line:
ANSWER: <answer>

Count the records satisfying glow >= 3.
Records:

  - F: mass 4, glow 8, span 7, charge 8
  - K: mass 7, glow 5, span 5, charge 6
  - W: mass 7, glow 3, span 1, charge 4
  - R: mass 8, glow 5, span 8, charge 8
  - M: mass 4, glow 2, span 6, charge 5
End with exactly one line:
ANSWER: <answer>

Maintain three registers. Initial state: gex=2, bek=4, son=2. Apply each instruction to the current state in order.
  1. cap gex at 4
  2. swap the values of son and gex
  3. if son is at least 8, add 2 to gex
  4. move up to 1 from gex to son
  5. swap the values of gex and son
End with exactly one line:
ANSWER: <answer> (format name=value entries in the original name order, separated by ';')

Choose the single probe that best distinguishes these three candidate rules. Score a probe by how many distinct outputs the hypotheses predict; choose the unique maximum.
Hypotheses:
  H1: overwrite position 4 with P
  H2: if the first item is H or M, rotate left once; otherwise rotate right once
  H3: if the first item is A or H, rotate left once; otherwise rotate right once
Candidate probes:
  P1: A / A / J / H
  P2: H / M / P / W
  P3: H / D / W / D
Cycle order: A P R H J M D W (after the last item, wrap to the first).
End with exactly one line: ANSWER: P1, ANSWER: P2, or ANSWER: P3

Follow exactly 6 pointers, starting at sex.
  - sex points to bik
  - tar points to nor
  - nor points to kin
  - pot points to sex
  - kin points to pot
  - gok points to tar
  - bik points to gok
End with exactly one line:
ANSWER: <answer>

Apply this procedure in order to 13 · 24 · 13 · 13 · 91:
  overwrite position 5 with 34; then reverse the entire sequence; then if the first item is 12 or 13, rotate left once; otherwise rotate right once; then reverse the entire sequence.
Cycle order: 02 24 34 12 91 46 13 (after the last item, wrap to the first).
End with exactly one line:
ANSWER: <answer>

A hidden rule transforms sequences of 7 items by chaining exactly two operations. Allowed operations are cycle-advance, full reversal, a fixed-position swap, a fixed-position overwrite, and a first-item-conditional one-step rotation. The examples determine the requested output.
Cycle order: R A K H W T P M (after the last item, wrap to the first).
Probe log:
  K / A / K / W / R / K / A -> M / P / M / A / M / M / P
  A / K / W / K / P / T / H -> P / M / A / M / M / K / R
  R / A / R / M / R / K / A -> T / P / T / W / M / M / P
  K / T / T / T / K / A / T -> M / K / K / K / M / P / K
  R / W / T / H / M / W / W -> T / A / K / R / M / A / A
  M / T / M / W / M / R / R -> W / K / W / A / M / T / T

Find the output for A / P / T / R / A / R / R.
End with exactly one line:
ANSWER: <answer>

Recover the unique complete order implied by these precedence facts.
  - P occurs before T
  - M occurs before R
  - J occurs before C
  - C occurs before P
  - R occurs before J
  - F occurs before M
